news.summary: A popular pet supplement brand in the UK, YuMOVE, recently transitioned its subscriber experience to Ordergroove's enterprise platform based in New York. This move aims to elevate the experience of its vast subscriber base, which spans hundreds of thousands of individuals. By making this strategic change, YuMOVE anticipates an improvement in operational efficiency, paving the way for sustained and profitable growth. The brand found the need for a solution that could align with its expansion objectives and cater to the evolving needs of its customers, following various challenges encountered earlier.
news.headline: YuMOVE, UK's top pet supplement brand, shifts to Ordergroove platform to improve experience for hundreds of thousands of subscribers.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, in Southampton, Pennsylvania, Quaint Oak Bancorp, Inc. (OTCQB: QNTO) declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.13 per share on its common stock. The dividend was announced by the holding company for Quaint Oak Bank on April 9, 2025, and will be payable on May 5, 2025. Shareholders of record at the close of business on April 21, 2025, will receive the dividend.
news.headline: Quaint Oak Bancorp declares $0.13 quarterly dividend on April 9, 2025, payable on May 5, 2025, to shareholders of record.

news.summary: Charleston, South Carolina - ATS has been honored with an annual award for being one of Lineage's top transportation service providers, in recognition of their exceptional service quality and operational excellence.
news.headline: ATS Intermodal awarded Lineage 2024 Carrier of the Year Award for exceptional service and operations.

news.summary: On Thursday, despite softer inflation and a temporary trade truce by President Trump, U.S. Treasury yields rose, indicating investor skepticism towards a Fed pivot and prolonged policy uncertainty. The 30-year Treasury note yield surged 8 basis points to 4.81%, recovering half of the previous day's decline due to the tariff pause. The 10-year yield increased by 3 basis points to 4.35%, despite positive macroeconomic signals. The iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (TLT) fell 1.5%. Inflation fell more than expected in March 2025, with CPI dropping to 2.4%, the lowest since September 2024, down from 2.8% in February. Monthly inflation also contracted by 0.1%, the first negative reading since May 2020. Bond investors are cautious, awaiting a clearer signal from the Fed.
news.headline: Treasury yields rose despite soft inflation data, with 30-year yield hitting 4.81% and 10-year yield at 4.35%. Market remains skeptical of Fed pivot. Inflation data shows CPI dropped more than expected, with annual rate at 2.4%, marking a significant decrease. Bond investors are waiting for clearer signal from the Fed amidst rising uncertainty.

news.summary: In a recent podcast, prominent technical analyst Kevin suggests that the current market turbulence may be signaling an imminent reversal following a predictable pattern. He noted that his three-month-old forecast predicted a correction lasting between 114 and 174 days, with the market now entering the critical "reversal zone." Kevin downplayed the panic caused by Trump's tariff announcements, labeling it as a media-driven emotional overreaction. He pointed out that every prediction he made is unfolding, indicating that tariff fears are subsiding and technical indicators align with historical precedents. The news that tariffs are paused has contributed to this sentiment.
news.headline: Bitcoin is back above $80,000 as technical analyst predicts upcoming reversal amid market turbulence following historical patterns.
Fed's Schmid: The US is entering a challenging period from a position of strength, the labor market has remained robust.

news.summary: Prominent figures in the financial industry are beginning to resist President Donald Trump's tariffs, with some Wall Street leaders expressing concern about the impact of these policies. Despite their efforts, there is doubt within the industry about the effectiveness of their opposition.
news.headline: Stock market impacted by trade war chaos as Wall Street leaders criticize President Trump's tariff policies but remain skeptical of impact.

news.summary: On April 10, 2025, in Reston, Virginia, Rafael Systems Global Sustainment (RSGS) announced the appointment of Jennifer Swanson to its Board of Directors. With more than 33 years of experience in government service, Jennifer brings expertise in defense procurement and digital strategies to the company. This addition strengthens the board with her valuable insights and leadership.

news.summary: CyberArk, a company based in New York, is collaborating with Accenture from Newton, Massachusetts to combine Accenture's AI Refinery™ with CyberArk's AI-enhanced Identity Security Platform for improved security measures. The partnership aims to leverage the expertise of the two companies to enhance the security features of CyberArk's platform. This integration signifies a strategic move by both companies to harness the power of artificial intelligence in strengthening identity security.
news.headline: CyberArk partners with Accenture to enhance identity security for AI agents using AI Refinery.

news.summary: Vortex Companies, a leading provider of trenchless infrastructure solutions, has recently announced its acquisition of an environmental services and trenchless solutions provider in the UK and Ireland. This move, completed by the 53-year-old company, aims to strengthen Vortex's presence in these regions. Widely recognized for its rapid growth, Vortex continues to expand its global reach. The acquisition is expected to have a significant impact on the company's operations in the UK and Ireland, consolidating its position as a key player in the industry.
news.headline: Vortex acquires McAllister Group to boost UK presence, expanding trenchless solutions; founded 53 years ago.

news.summary: In Denver on April 10, 2025, Ping Identity revealed their enhanced focus on supporting partners and customers through strategic investments. news.summary: A report on the global market for 3D Scanners has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings, indicating a projected increase in demand to US$8.8 billion by 2030. This is a significant rise from the estimated market size of US$4.9 billion in 2024. The compound annual growth rate (CAGR) for 3D Scanners is expected to be 10.2% between 2024 and 2030. The report provides an analysis of the market concerning scanner type, installation type, range, application, and end-use sector for various regional and country markets from 2021 to 2030.
news.headline: Global 3D Scanners market projected to reach $8.8 billion by 2030 from estimated $4.9 billion in 2024, growing at CAGR of 10.2%.

news.summary: An inventor from South River, New Jersey, introduced a new product called the E Z - P B & J on April 10, 2025. This innovative creation offers a convenient and mess-free way to enjoy peanut butter and jelly. The inventor aimed to streamline the process and eliminate the hassle of using jars and knives. The E Z - P B & J is designed to save time and enhance the experience of enjoying this classic combination. The product's release has garnered attention and is expected to revolutionize the way people consume PB & J.
news.headline: Inventor from South River, N.J. creates E Z - P B & J packaging for peanut butter & jelly, saves time & reduces mess.

news.summary: An optical switch startup called nEye Systems, established by UC Berkeley technologists, has recently wrapped up a Series B funding round amounting to $58 million. The leading investor in this round was Alphabet’s independent growth fund, CapitalG, joined by M12 - Microsoft’s venture fund, Micron Ventures, NVIDIA, and Socratic Partners. With this recent investment, nEye has accumulated a total funding of $72.5 million, combining this Series B with an earlier undisclosed Series A that was helmed by TEDA Holdings.
news.headline: nEye Systems secures $72.5M to innovate AI infrastructure using optical switch tech. $58M in Series B led by CapitalG.

news.summary: In Los Angeles, HopSkipDrive, a tech company addressing transportation issues with a focus on safety, access, and care, revealed the addition of Dave Lissy to its Board of Directors. Lissy, previously the CEO of Bright Horizons Family Solutions (NYSE: BFAM) for 16 years until 2018, brings a wealth of experience in education and care to the role. Under his tenure, Bright Horizons solidified its position as a top provider of early education services.
news.headline: HopSkipDrive appoints Dave Lissy, former CEO of Bright Horizons, to its Board of Directors for transportation safety.

news.summary: Anna Bublitz from Overland Park, Kansas, has won the grand prize valued at over $700,000 in the HGTV Urban Oasis 2024 contest. The announcement was made by HGTV, a part of the Warner Bros. Discovery group of companies, on April 10, 2025.
news.headline: Local winner Anna Bublitz of Kansas City, Missouri, wins 2024 HGTV Urban Oasis valued at over $700,000.

news.summary: In Arlington, Virginia, the prominent health intelligence network, Surescripts®, revealed that its technology and data infrastructure systems have received certified status from HITRUST for information security. The HITRUST r2 Certification highlights that Surescripts' systems have successfully met rigorous regulatory compliance and industry-specific standards, ensuring effective risk management. This recognition positions Surescripts as a leader in safeguarding health information.

news.summary: In New York, the rapidly evolving business landscape is placing immense pressure on go-to-market teams to adapt quickly. Previously successful marketing strategies have become ineffective, and once reliable sales playbooks are now obsolete. Customer service demands have surged beyond manageable levels for most teams. The rapid adoption of AI technology is fueling these changes, promising transformation but also overwhelming small and medium-sized businesses. Three out of four leaders concede feeling overwhelmed by the influx of AI tools and updates, highlighting the urgent need for businesses to navigate and leverage these advancements to stay competitive.
news.headline: HubSpot unveils new AI agents and 200+ updates at Spring 2025 Spotlight, aiding SMBs overwhelmed by AI tools.

news.summary: In Wilmington, Delaware on April 9, 2025, a recent shift in the home appliance market due to new U.S. tariffs on imported goods has prompted Appliances 4 Less (A4L) to make strategic changes. A4L is set to undergo a major expansion across the country, and it will also be altering its approach to handling returned and overstock appliances. The move comes as a response to the changing market dynamics following the implementation of the tariffs. This expansion will see A4L increase its presence at numerous locations nationwide, with a noteworthy focus on optimizing operations related to returned and excess stock items.
news.headline: Appliances 4 Less expands nationwide due to 2025 tariff changes, altering supply chain model.

news.summary: In Westborough, Massachusetts, eClinicalWorks revealed its collaboration with Oak Orchard Health, an 83-provider FQHC, in using the Sunoh.ai AI medical scribe and healow patient engagement solutions. news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 10, 2025, an inventor from Baton Rouge, Louisiana shared details about their creation, the BALCONY BLINDS. The inventor expressed their motivation to enhance privacy on balconies through a simple and decorative solution. This unique alternative to standard balcony blinds aims to fulfill this need and offer a new perspective on balcony privacy.

news.summary: In Santa Clara, California, Lisa Harrup Mieuli has been appointed as the Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) for Gigamon. She will be responsible for leading global marketing strategy, demand generation efforts, and enhancing brand awareness for the company.

news.summary: TNL Mediagene, a renowned media and technology company in Asia, disclosed its preliminary unaudited financial results for the year ended December 31, 2024. Their revenue surged by over 30% year-over-year, a boost credited to diversification into tech and data-based products, such as retail media networks and strategic data partnerships. The growth also stemmed from innovative content leading to heightened user engagement, especially in short-form video formats. Adjusted EBITDA stood near profitability, excluding extraordinary items like DeSPAC and IPO fees, and currency fluctuations. CEO Chung highlighted the successful year in revenue and EBITDA margin improvement, emphasizing further diversification of operations and products across different languages, including data analytics and AI services for a promising outlook in 2025. (Word count: 119)
news.headline: TNL Mediagene reports a strong 2024 with over 30% revenue growth and plans for continued growth in 2025. CEO optimistic about diversification.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 10, 2025, an inventor from Catonsville, Maryland, expressed frustration with the wastefulness of using paper and money to cover tables for a seafood broil. This inspired the creation of the CRAB PAD, a reusable table cover. The inventor's design addresses this issue and offers a sustainable solution. This innovation aims to reduce paper waste and costs associated with traditional table coverings for events like seafood broils.
news.headline: Inventor creates reusable table cover CRAB PAD for seafood broils, aiming to reduce waste - PRNewswire, April 10, 2025.

news.summary: In Miami, The Hackett Group, Inc., a prominent generative artificial intelligence (Gen AI) consultancy and advisory firm listed on NASDAQ as HCKT, has shared insights from their 2025 Key Issues Study. The study highlights that AI is reshaping procurement, with 64% of procurement leaders anticipating AI and Gen AI to revolutionize their responsibilities within the next five years. However, the procurement sector is facing a significant obstacle as workloads are expected to surge by 10% in 2025.
news.headline: 64% of procurement leaders foresee AI transforming their roles in 5 years per The Hackett Group - workloads set to rise by 10% in 2025.

news.summary: Redding, California - On April 10, 2025, a market analysis revealed a surge in market growth attributed to the increasing need for energy-saving solutions and the incorporation of AI and IoT in household devices. The report, "Smart Appliances Market," focuses on various product categories such as kitchen appliances, laundry appliances, and HVAC systems. The market is experiencing substantial growth, with key factors including a shift towards energy efficiency and the adoption of advanced technologies. The integration of AI and IoT has played a significant role in transforming traditional appliance functionalities, leading to a considerable rise in demand. This trend indicates a promising future for the market, with projected significant growth in the coming years.
news.headline: Smart appliances market to hit $93.03B by 2032, growing at 11.5% CAGR from 2025. Demand for energy-efficient solutions and integration of AI & IoT drive growth.

news.summary: In preparation for the 2025 Setouchi Triennale in Naoshima, Japan, a new digital platform called "Finding Naoshima" has been launched. This platform serves as the first English-language travel guide specifically for Naoshima, aiming to enhance the experience for international tourists. The initiative addresses the information gap for global travelers as Naoshima gains popularity worldwide. The Setouchi Triennale, scheduled for 2025, will showcase the island's renowned art scene, with significant numbers of visitors expected to attend.
news.headline: "First English travel guide for Naoshima launches ahead of 2025 Setouchi Triennale, bridging information gap for global travelers."
